title: Podman failed behind the proxy
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo yum install podman

2. podman run hello-world


**Describe the results you received:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5471356/105388954-8cbafd00-5c17-11eb-908e-87b880fe131a.png)


**Describe the results you expected:**
Hello world

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Running on Centos7
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Barematal system

comments.body: Can you provide details on your proxy configuration - what environment variables are set, at a minimum.
Hello @mheon . I exported http_proxy, https_proxy and all_proxy in the environment before running `podman run hello-world`

Go language handles this, so not something that can be fixed in Podman.
@mtrmac @vrothberg Ideas?
`proxyconnect tcp`: means that it is actually connecting to something. See what’s going on using something like Wireshark?

@rhatdan @mtrmac  I have both Singularity and Docker working perfectly fine on this system. Singularity runs on Go. Is there a way to setup the proxies for Podman if it doesn't take the system proxies?
As of now, this is still an issue that is preventing me from using Podman. The admin and many users don't have any issues working with other apps in the lab. So it's clearely a Podman issue.

Without further details on proxy configuration I don't know if we can help. Podman does not perform any proxy configuration, or take any action at all on proxies - it is all handled by the Go standard library. With further details, a bug could be filed against the Go standard library (we've had to do this before on proxy handling).

Any chance could you check if buildah/skopeo have the same problems?  If they work, and Podman does not then we could do further investigation.  If they don't then the issue is either in your setup, Golang or containers/image.
@Smahane ping, have you had a chance to try buildah or skopeo to see if they present the same issues?

I'm not familiar with buildah/skopeo. So far I'm getting this error bellow. I will need to google a bit

(base) bash-4.2$ buildah pull docker-daemon:hello-world
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
User namespaces are not enabled in /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces.
ERRO error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
ERRO (unable to determine exit status)

You are on a RHEL7 box and have to enable the /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces in order to use user namespace for rootless podman or rootless buildah.

Could you try to do these commands as root, and see if they work with your proxy?
Friendly ping. @Smahane, are you still seeing the issue?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
